Nazi Looted Booksat Penn Libraries

ScopeThe methodThe five stepsWhat we cannot account forWhere to look
/markings/

The markings

Every kind of mark found in these books, and how often it appears across the holdings. For where in the book each one turns up — including the places nobody looks — see where to look.

There are three principal forms of provenance evidence — an OAD stamp, a JCR bookplate, and a gutter source note — and a book may carry all three, or only one. On top of these, a book may bear one of more than a dozen Nazi confiscation stamps from the two offices that did the looting: the ERR (Rosenberg’s task force, gathering books for a planned institute in Frankfurt) and the RSHA (the SS security office that competed with it). A Nazi stamp alone is enough to prove a book was looted. RSHA stamps are essentially absent from this collection; the Nazi mark that does appear is the ERR’s.

OAD stamp
Offenbach Archival Depot
Title page, its verso, and the last-page verso
435 books→
JCR bookplate
Jewish Cultural Reconstruction
Front pastedown
1042 books→
Gutter source note
“Gift of JCR”
Gutter behind the title page
1016 books→
ERR stamp
Einsatzstab Reichsleiter Rosenberg
Title page and front pastedown
4 books→
Johannes Pohl pastedown
ERR (Pohl)
Inside the upper board
41 books→
Disinfection stamp
Offenbach intake
Flyleaf and title-page verso
93 books→
AJDC Library stamp
the “Joint”
Inside back board (lower) / rear cover
17 books→
Pre-war owner stamps
personal & institutional
Throughout — title page, boards, flyleaf
1034 books→
Provenance research & text: Bruce Nielsen · Penn Libraries, Katz Center for Advanced Judaic StudiesUncertainty in this record is shown, not hidden.
